question_text: The paired organ located at the lower right side of the final segmented image, which is larger than the right kidney, can produce up to 170-180 liters of filtrate per day.

The liver occupies a large area on the left side of the image. Please segment one of the relatively small paired organs located centrally in the image, whose parenchyma is divided into cortex and medulla and functions in electrolyte regulation.

Now segment one of the paired organs located in the lower left side of the image, which is significantly smaller in volume than the liver. Its parenchyma is divided into cortex and medulla, responsible for filtration and reabsorption.
Next, segment the smaller symmetrical organ located at the lower right side of the image, inferior to the right kidney. This organ secretes erythropoietin.
Please segment the organ on the left side of the image that is involved in hormone inactivation. Impairment of this organ's function may lead to sex hormone imbalance.

Please locate and segment the largest gland in the human body on the left side of the image. This structure is mobile during respiration and should not normally extend beyond the lower edge of the costal margin.

Please segment the organ on the left side of the image that is involved in vitamin metabolism. This organ participates in the synthesis and storage of various vitamins and plays a crucial role in hormone inactivation. Impaired function may lead to vitamin deficiencies and sex hormone imbalances.

Please segment the smaller organ on the left side of the image. This organ maintains fluid balance through water reabsorption in the renal tubules, and its functional units consist of renal corpuscles and renal tubules.

Now please segment another organ located on the left side of the image, which is smaller than the liver and associated with the "congenital foundation" in traditional Chinese medicine theory, responsible for regulating acid-base balance of body fluids.

Please locate a structure in the lower right side of the image that resembles the right kidney but is smaller. This organ reabsorbs most of the primary urine through renal tubules and excretes nitrogenous waste.
Please segment the organ on the left side of the image that has vitamin metabolism function and is involved in water-electrolyte balance regulation.
Please segment the hematopoietic organ on the left side of the image. This organ's hematopoietic function degenerates in adulthood but may resume under certain pathological conditions.
Please segment the organ on the left side of the image that is involved in hormone inactivation, as dysfunction of this organ may lead to abnormal sexual characteristics.

Finally, segment the small lymphatic organ on the right side of the image that is smaller than the kidney, adjacent to the gastric fundus and left kidney, and divided into diaphragmatic and visceral surfaces.
Please segment the important organ on the left side of the image that is responsible for vitamin metabolism and thermal energy supply.
Please segment the larger organ in the central region of the image, which has the important functions of bile secretion and detoxification.
Now please segment another organ on the left side of the image that is smaller than the liver, which maintains fluid homeostasis by regulating water and electrolyte balance.

Now please segment another organ located on the left side of the image, which is slightly larger than the liver. Its functional units consist of renal corpuscles and renal tubules, responsible for excreting nitrogenous waste.
There is a small organ located on the left side of the liver, with distinct cortical and medullary structures, responsible for blood filtration and urine production. Please segment this structure.
Next, segment the left counterpart organ adjacent to the right kidney but smaller in volume, whose nephron structures are responsible for urine production.
Now please segment another organ on the left side of the image, which is larger than the liver and has a capsule composed of renal fascia, adipose capsule, and fibrous capsule.
Please segment the organ on the left side of the image that has bile secretion function and is often associated with jaundice symptoms in diseases.
Below the liver, segment an organ that is significantly smaller in size but functions in regulating body fluid osmotic pressure and endocrine activities.

Now please segment another organ located on the left side of the image, which is slightly smaller than the liver. Its main functions include regulating body fluid osmotic pressure and secreting erythropoietin.
Compared to the right kidney, there is another similar organ in the central region but with a larger area, which also functions to filter blood.
Please segment the organ on the left side of the image that has important functions in synthesizing coagulation factors and is also involved in water metabolism regulation and acid-base balance.

Now please segment the organ located on the right side of the image that is significantly smaller than the liver. The splenic hilum structure of this organ serves as the critical entry and exit point for blood vessels and nerves.
Please segment the largest glandular organ on the left side of the image, which is involved in water metabolism and acid-base balance regulation.
Now please segment the organ on the right side of the image that has the same function as the right kidney but is located opposite to it, which is slightly smaller than the right kidney.
Please segment the metabolically active organ on the left side of the image, which serves hematopoietic functions during fetal development and produces bile in adults.
There is also an organ on the right side of the image with similar function to the right kidney but a smaller area. Please segment this structure.

Please segment the organ located in the upper left abdomen of the image. This organ has hematopoietic function during fetal development and may reactivate this function under certain pathological conditions in adults. Its lesions are often accompanied by jaundice symptoms.
Compared to the right kidney, there is another slightly larger symmetrical organ on the right side of the image. Please segment this structure responsible for acid-base balance regulation.
Another left-sided organ is visible medial and inferior to the liver, with a larger area responsible for regulating osmotic pressure balance of body fluids. Please segment this structure.
Now please segment the organ in the lower right corner of the image. It is smaller than the liver and serves as one of the primary sites for lymphocyte residence.
Finally, segment the smallest lymphatic organ on the right side of the image. Its parenchyma is divided into white pulp and red pulp, which serves as the primary site for specific immune responses.
Please segment the organ on the left side of the image that has detoxification function and participates in water metabolism regulation.
Now please segment a smaller organ in the center of the image, which is located below the liver and is responsible for regulating fluid balance and osmotic pressure in the human body.
Please segment the organ on the left side of the image that is involved in hormone inactivation, as dysfunction of this organ may lead to sex hormone imbalance.

Next, please segment the lymphatic organ located between the liver and the left kidney on the right side. This structure contains abundant blood sinuses and is highly susceptible to mechanical injury.
Now please segment the much smaller lymphoid organ located on the right side of the image compared to the liver, which serves as an important site for immune responses.

On the right side of the image, the smallest organ can be observed, with its marginal zone serving antigen recognition functions and possibly accompanied by an accessory spleen. Please segment this structure.

Please segment the organ on the left side of the image that is internally and externally related to the gallbladder and primarily stores blood. In traditional Chinese medicine theory, it opens into the eyes.
Please segment the important organ on the left side of the image that is involved in water metabolism and heat energy supply, which can regulate acid-base balance and mineral metabolism.
Please segment the larger parenchymal organ on the left side of the image, which is primarily responsible for metabolic functions and bile synthesis.
Please segment the larger metabolic organ on the left side of the image, which is primarily responsible for deoxidation, synthesis of secretory proteins, and bile production.

Finally, segment the organ on the right side of the image, which is larger than the right kidney and responsible for excreting nitrogenous waste and drugs. Its proximal tubule is the primary site for the excretion of organic acids.
Finally, segment the smallest lymphoid organ on the right side of the image, whose long axis aligns with the 10th rib and is rich in B cells.

Next, please segment the symmetrical organ on the right side of the image that is similar in size to the right kidney. It is the functional unit of urine production, composed of nephrons and collecting ducts.

Compared to the liver, the organ in the lower right area of the image is significantly smaller in size. It is the largest lymphoid organ in the human body, involved in immune functions and blood storage.
Please segment the endocrine-functional organ located at the lower left side of the image, which secretes important hormones such as renin and erythropoietin.
Next, please segment the organ located at the center of the image, which is slightly smaller than the right kidney and primarily responsible for regulating body water and osmotic pressure balance.
Please locate and segment the organ on the left side of the image that is internally and externally related to the gallbladder and opens into the eyes.

Please segment the endocrine organ on the left side of the image that regulates fluid balance. According to traditional Chinese medicine theory, it is internally and externally related to the bladder and governs bones and marrow production.

Please segment the urinary system organ on the left side of the image that has excretory and reabsorptive functions. Its parenchyma consists of cortex and medulla, and it can form primitive urine through glomerular filtration.
Finally, segment the immune organ located between the two kidneys on the right side of the image, characterized by red pulp structure.
Please segment the important organ on the left side of the image that is involved in water metabolism and acid-base balance regulation, which is adjacent to the stomach and duodenum.
Please locate and segment the organ on the left side of the image that is primarily involved in metabolic and detoxification functions.
Next, segment the organ located inferiorly with an area larger than the right kidney. This organ excretes nitrogenous waste through glomerular filtration and reabsorption.

Now, please segment the organ on the right side of the image that is significantly smaller than the liver. This organ is the primary site of peripheral immune responses and has characteristic notches on its surface.

In the lower left region of the liver image, there is a larger organ. Please segment this structure, whose capsule consists of the renal fascia, adipose capsule, and fibrous capsule from outer to inner layers.
An organ on the same side as the right kidney but positioned higher, with a characteristic splenic hilum structure on its visceral surface, involved in immune regulation.

Now please segment another organ on the left side of the image. It is smaller than the liver, with its parenchyma divided into two layers: cortex and medulla. It exists in pairs but is slightly larger on the left side.

Now please segment another organ located on the left side of the image, which is much smaller than the liver. Its primary function is to regulate fluid balance and osmotic pressure. In traditional Chinese medicine theory, it is regarded as the foundation of innate constitution.
Please segment the parenchymal organ in the central region of the image that has hematopoietic function and vitamin metabolism function.

Please segment a larger solid organ on the left side of the image. Although this organ is not a primary hematopoietic organ in adulthood, it can resume hematopoietic function under certain pathological conditions. It also serves as an important thermogenic organ in the body.
Now please segment another organ on the left side of the image, which is slightly larger than the liver, has endocrine functions, and can regulate calcium-phosphorus metabolism and erythropoiesis.
Please segment the organ on the left side of the image that has the function of secreting and excreting bile and is internally-externally related to the gallbladder.

Please segment the organ on the left side of the image that is involved in water metabolism and acid-base balance regulation. It is located in the right hypochondrium and epigastrium, adjacent to the right lung and heart.

Now please segment the small lymphatic organ located at the lower right side of the image, which is smaller than the liver. It is the largest lymphoid tissue in the human body and functions in blood storage and immune response.
Please segment the detoxifying organ on the left side of the image, which transforms and eliminates toxic substances from the body.
Inferior and slightly to the left of the liver, another smaller organ is visible. Its capsule is composed of renal fascia and adipose capsule, with nephrons serving as its functional units.

Please locate and segment the important organ on the left side of the image that is involved in metabolic and detoxification functions. This organ synthesizes bile and participates in hormone inactivation, and long-term functional impairment may lead to endocrine disorders.
Please segment the organ on the left side of the image that has hematopoietic function and vitamin metabolism function.
Please segment the main organ on the left side of the image, which is internally and externally related to the gallbladder in traditional Chinese medicine theory and has hematopoietic potential.
Please segment the organ on the left side of the image involved in hormone inactivation, as its impaired function may lead to sex hormone imbalance.
Please segment the important organ in the central region of the image that is involved in water metabolism and detoxification functions.

Please segment the largest gland in the central region of the image. This organ is internally and externally related to the gallbladder in Traditional Chinese Medicine theory and has the function of storing blood.
Please segment the larger metabolic organ on the left side of the image, which is responsible for producing bile in the digestive system and involved in the synthesis and storage of various vitamins.
Please segment the larger solid organ on the left side of the image, which has hematopoietic function during fetal development and participates in energy metabolism in adults.
Please segment the larger organ on the left side of the image, which plays a significant role in energy metabolism and fat digestion, and secretes bile.
Now segment the organ in the lower right of the image. It is smaller than the liver, a key component of the lymphatic system, and prone to traumatic rupture.

Next, segment the organ in the lower right corner of the image. It is slightly larger than the right kidney, has extremely rich blood flow, and can filter a large volume of fluid daily while maintaining pH balance by regulating acid-base ions.
There is a lymphatic organ on the right side of the image, with an area between that of the liver and kidney, which is prone to damage from external forces. Please segment this structure.

Now please segment another organ on the left side of the image, which is slightly larger than the liver. Its parenchyma consists of cortex and medulla, and its blood flow accounts for a significant proportion of total body blood flow.
Compared to the right kidney, segment the larger organ of the same type located directly below the image, whose proximal tubules primarily perform reabsorption functions.
Please locate and segment the vital organ in the central region of the image that is internally and externally connected with the gallbladder and opens into the eyes.

Finally, segment another organ on the right side of the image, which is slightly smaller in area than the left kidney. It is an important peripheral immune organ in the human body, with a characteristic splenic hilum structure on its visceral surface.
Please segment the organ on the left side of the image that regulates acid-base balance and maintains fluid stability.

Please locate and segment the organ on the left side of the image that is internally and externally related to the gallbladder. This organ functions in storing blood, regulating discharge, and participates in fluid metabolism regulation.

Please segment the organ on the left side of the image that is internally and externally related to the gallbladder, has the functions of storing blood and regulating flow, and opens into the eyes according to traditional Chinese medicine theory.
Now please segment the organ located in the lower right corner of the image. It is much smaller than the liver and has functions in regulating blood pressure and erythropoiesis.
Please segment the organ in the central region of the image that has secretory and detoxification functions.
In the lower right side of the image, there is a lymphatic organ larger than the left kidney, whose red pulp serves as the primary site for immune phagocytosis. Please segment this structure.

Please segment the largest glandular structure on the left side of the image. This organ is internally and externally related to the gallbladder in Traditional Chinese Medicine theory, primarily responsible for storing blood and regulating discharge functions.

Now please segment another organ on the left side of the image that is smaller in area than the liver. Its capsular structure includes the adipose capsule and renal fascia, and it participates in acid-base balance regulation.

Now please segment another organ on the left side of the image, which is slightly larger than the liver. It is primarily responsible for filtering blood and forming urine, with the proximal tubule being its key functional site.
Please segment the main metabolic organ in the central region of the image, which is involved in important physiological functions such as protein synthesis and bile secretion.
The left kidney is located on the right side of the image. Now please segment the ipsilateral larger lymphatic organ, which functions in blood filtration and immune response.
Now please segment the organ located on the right side of the image, which is significantly smaller than the liver and serves as the primary filtration site for circulating blood in the body.

Please segment the organ on the left side of the image that is internally and externally related to the gallbladder and governs blood storage metabolism. In traditional Chinese medicine theory, this organ also opens into the eyes.

Now please segment another organ on the left side of the image, which is larger than the liver and has the functions of regulating fluid balance and filtering blood, with clearly distinct cortical and medullary structures.

Please segment the organ on the left side of the image that is internally and externally connected with the gallbladder, has the functions of storing blood and regulating flow, and also secretes bile to promote fat digestion.
